2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Has taught the Qur’an to His beloved Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him).

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Has taught (you mankind) the Quran (by His Mercy).

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: It is He Who has taught the Qur'an.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Has created Prophet Mohammed (peace and blessings be upon him) as the soul of mankind.

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: And taught him to express clearly.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: Has taught him the knowledge of the past and the future.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: and He has taught him the Explanation.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: and has taught him articulate speech.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: and has taught him intelligible speech.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Taught him the mode of expression.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: He has taught him speech (and intelligence).

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: The sun and moon revolve to a computation;

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: The sun and the moon are scheduled.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: The sun and the moon to a reckoning,

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: The sun and the moon are in a reckoning.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: The sun and the moon run on their fixed courses (exactly) calculated with measured out stages for each (for reckoning, etc.).

5.author: Itani
5.text: The sun and the moon move according to plan.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: The sun and the moon follow a reckoning,

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: The sun and the moon (run) on fixed courses.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: The sun and the moon are made punctual.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: The sun and the moon are [disposed] calculatedly,

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: The sun and the moon to a reckoning.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: The sun and the moon [move] by precise calculation,

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: The sun and moon rotate in a predestined orbit.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: The sun and the moon follow a reckoning.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: The sun and the moon move according to a fixed reckoning;

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: The sun and the moon follow courses (exactly) computed;

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: And the grasses and the trees bow (to Him) in adoration.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And the plants and the trees prostrate (for Him).

6.author: Arberry
6.text: and the stars and the trees bow themselves;

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: And the herbs and the trees do obe isance."

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: And the herbs (or stars) and the trees both prostrate.

6.author: Itani
6.text: And the stars and the trees prostrate themselves.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: and the stars and the trees all prostrate themselves,

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: And the Najm and the trees prostrating.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: The stars and the trees prostrate.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: and the herb and the tree prostrate [to Allah].

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: The stars and the trees prostrate themselves.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And the stars and trees prostrate.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: The plants and trees prostrate before Him.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And the herbs and the trees do prostrate (to Him).

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: the stars and the trees bend in prostration.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: And the herbs and the trees - both (alike) prostrate in adoration.

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: He raised the sky and set the Balance

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And Allah has raised the sky; and He has set the balance.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: and heaven -- He raised it up, and set the Balance.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And the heaven! He hath elevated it, and He hath set the balance.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And the heaven He has raised high, and He has set up the Balance.

7.author: Itani
7.text: And the sky, He raised; and He set up the balance.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: and He has raised up the heaven and has set a balance

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: And the heaven He has raised high, and He has set up the balance.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And the sky He hath uplifted; and He hath set the measure,

7.author: Qarai
7.text: He raised the heaven high and set up the balance,

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: He raised the heaven on high and set the scale.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And the heaven He raised and imposed the balance

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: He raised the heavens and set up everything in balance,

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And the heaven, He raised it high, and He made the balance

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: He raised the heavens and set up the measure,

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: And the Firmament has He raised high, and He has set up the Balance (of Justice),

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: So that none may err against the scales,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: In order that you may not corrupt the balance.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: That ye should not trespass in respect of the balance.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: In order that you may not transgress (due) balance.

8.author: Itani
8.text: So do not transgress in the balance.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: that you may not transgress in the balance,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: In order that you may not transgress the balance.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: That ye exceed not the measure,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: That you not transgress within the balance.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: so that you would maintain justice.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: That you may not be inordinate in respect of the measure.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: so that you should not transgress the measure.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: In order that ye may not transgress (due) balance.

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: And observe correct measure, weigh with justice, and not cheat the balance.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And establish the measures justly, nor decrease the due weight.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: and weigh with justice, and skimp not in the Balance.)

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And observe the weight with equity, and make not deficient the balance.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And observe the weight with equity and do not make the balance deficient.

9.author: Itani
9.text: But maintain the weights with justice, and do not violate the balance.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: but weigh things equitably and skimp not in the balance.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And observe the weight with equity and do not make the balance deficient.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: But observe the measure strictly, nor fall short thereof.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Maintain the weights with justice, and do not shorten the balance!’

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: Give just weight and do not skimp the scales.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And establish weight in justice and do not make deficient the balance.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Therefore, maintain just measure and do not transgress against the Balance.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And keep up the balance with equity and do not make the measure deficient.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Always measure with justice and do not give short measure.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: So establish weight with justice and fall not short in the balance.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Waaqeemoo alwazna bialqisti wala tukhsiroo almeezana

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: He positioned the earth for all the creatures:

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And He appointed the earth for the creatures.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: And earth -- He set it down for all beings,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And the earth! He hath lain it out for the creatures.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And the earth He has put for the creatures.

10.author: Itani
10.text: And the earth; He set up for the creatures.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: And He has set up the earth for all beings.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And the earth He has put down (laid) for Al-An'am.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And the earth hath He appointed for (His) creatures,

10.author: Qarai
10.text: And the earth—He laid it out for mankind.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: He set down the earth for the creation.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And the earth He laid [out] for the creatures.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: He spread out the earth for the people.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And the earth, He has set it for living creatures;

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: He has laid out the earth for His creatures.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: It is He Who has spread out the earth for (His) creatures:

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: There are fruits of all kinds on it, and date-palms with their clusters sheathed,

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: In which are fruits, and covered dates.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: therein fruits, and palm-trees with sheaths,

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Therein are fruit and palm-trees sheathed.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Therein are fruits, date-palms producing sheathed fruit-stalks (enclosing dates).

11.author: Itani
11.text: In it are fruits, and palms in clusters.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: Therein are fruit and palm-trees with their dates in sheaths,

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Therein are fruits, date palms producing Akmam.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Wherein are fruit and sheathed palm-trees,

11.author: Qarai
11.text: In it are fruits and date-palms with sheaths,

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: Therein are fruits and palm trees with sheaths,

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Therein is fruit and palm trees having sheaths [of dates]

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: There exists all kinds of fruits, palm-trees with sheathed blossoms,

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Therein is fruit and palms having sheathed clusters,

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: On it are fruits and palm-trees with sheathed clusters [of dates],

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Therein is fruit and date-palms, producing spathes (enclosing dates);

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: Grain with husk, and fragrant grasses.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And grain covered with husk, and fragrant flowers.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: and grain in the blade, and fragrant herbs.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And grain chaffed and other food.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And also corn, with (its) leaves and stalk for fodder, and sweet-scented plants.

12.author: Itani
12.text: And grains in the blades, and fragrant plants.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: and a variety of corn with both husk and grain.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And also corn, with (its) `Asf, and Rayhan.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: grain with husk, and fragrant herbs.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: the grain in the blade; and aromatic herbs.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And grain having husks and scented plants.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: grain with its husk and aromatic herbs.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And the grain with (its) husk and fragrance.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: and grains with their husk and fragrant plants.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Also corn, with (its) leaves and stalk for fodder, and sweet-smelling plants.

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: How many favours of your Lord will then both of you deny?

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: So O men and jinns! Which favour of your Lord will you deny?

13.author: Arberry
13.text: O which of your Lord's bounties will you and you deny?

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: Which, then, of the benefits of your Lord, will ye twain belie?

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: Then which of the Blessings of your Lord will you both (jinns and men) deny?

13.author: Itani
13.text: So which of your Lord’s marvels will you deny?

13.author: Maududi
13.text: Which of the bounties of your Lord will you twain – you men and jinn – then deny?

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: Which is it, of the favours of your Lord, that ye deny?

13.author: Qarai
13.text: So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny?

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: Which favors of your Lord will you both (humans and jinn) belie?

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: (Jinn and mankind) - which of the favors of your Lord do you deny?

13.author: Shakir
13.text: Which then of the bounties of your Lord will you deny?

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: Which of your Lord's wonders would you deny?

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: Then which of the favours of your Lord will ye deny?

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: He created man of fermented clay dried tinkling hard like earthen ware,

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: He created man from clay like that of earthenware.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: He created man of a clay like the potter's,

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: He created man of clay like, Unto pottery.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: He created man (Adam) from sounding clay like the clay of pottery.

14.author: Itani
14.text: He created man from hard clay, like bricks.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: He has created man from dry, rotten clay like the potter's,

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: He created man from sounding clay like the clay of pottery.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: He created man of clay like the potter's,

14.author: Qarai
14.text: He created man out of dry clay, like the potter’s,

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: He created the human from clay, like earthenware

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: He created man from clay like [that of] pottery.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: He created the human being from clay like that used for pottery

14.author: Shakir
14.text: He created man from dry clay like earthen vessels,

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: He has created man, from dry ringing clay, like the potter"s,

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: He created man from sounding clay like unto pottery,

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: And created jinns from the white-hot flame of fire.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: And created jinn from the flame of fire.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: and He created the jinn of a smokeless fire.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And He created the Jinn of a flame of fire.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: And the jinns did He create from a smokeless flame of fire.

15.author: Itani
15.text: And created the jinn from a fusion of fire.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: and has created the jinn from the flame of fire.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And the Jinn He created from a smokeless flame of fire.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: And the jinn did He create of smokeless fire.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: and created the jinn out of a flame of a fire.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: and He created the jinn from smokeless fire.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And He created the jinn from a smokeless flame of fire.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: and jinn from the many colored flames of fire.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And He created the jinn of a flame of fire.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: and He created the jinns from a flame of fire.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: And He created Jinns from fire free of smoke:

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Wakhalaqa aljanna min marijin min narin

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: How many favours of your Lord will you then deny?

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: So O men and jinns! Which favour of your Lord will you deny?

16.author: Arberry
16.text: O which of your Lord's bounties will you and you deny?

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: Which, then, of the benefits of your Lord will ye twain belie

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Then which of the Blessings of your Lord will you both (jinns and men) deny?

16.author: Itani
16.text: So which of your Lord’s marvels will you deny?

16.author: Maududi
16.text: Which of the wonders of your Lord's power will you twain – you men and jinn – then deny?

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: Which is it, of the favours of your Lord, that ye deny?

16.author: Qarai
16.text: So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny?

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: Which favors of your Lord will you both belie?

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: (Jinn and mankind) - which of the favors of your Lord do you deny?

16.author: Shakir
16.text: Which then of the bounties of your Lord will you deny?

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: Which of your Lord's wonders would you deny?

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Then which of the favours of your Lord will ye deny?

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: Lord of the two Easts, Lord of the two Wests.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: Lord of both the Easts, and Lord of both the Wests!

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Lord of the Two Easts, Lord of the Two Wests,

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: He is Lord of the two easts and Lord of the two wests.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: (He is) the Lord of the two easts (places of sunrise during early summer and early winter) and the Lord of the two wests (places of sunset during early summer and early winter).

17.author: Itani
17.text: Lord of the two Easts and Lord of the two Wests.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Lord of the two easts and of the two wests is He.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: The Lord of the two easts and the Lord of the two wests.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Lord of the two Easts, and Lord of the two Wests!

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Lord of the two easts, and Lord of the two wests!

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: The Lord of the Two Easts, the Lord of the Two Wests.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: [He is] Lord of the two sunrises and Lord of the two sunsets.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: He is the Lord of the East and West through all seasons.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Lord of the East and Lord of the West.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: He is the Lord of the two easts and the Lord of the two wests.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: (He is) Lord of the two Easts and Lord of the two Wests:

18.text: How many favours of your Lord will then both of you deny?

18.text: So O men and jinns! Which favour of your Lord will you deny?

18.text: Then which of the Blessings of your Lord will you both (jinns and men) deny?

18.text: So which of your Lord’s marvels will you deny?

18.author: Maududi
18.text: Which of the powers of your Lord will you twain – you men and jinn – then deny?

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Which is it, of the favours of your Lord, that ye deny?

18.author: Qarai
18.text: So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny?

18.text: Which favors of your Lord will you both belie?

18.text: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?

18.text: Which then of the bounties of your Lord will you deny?

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: Which of your Lord's wonders would you deny?

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Then which of the favours of your Lord will ye deny?

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: He has set two seas in motion that flow side by side together,

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: He has set flowing two seas that appear to meet.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: He let forth the two seas that meet together,

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: He hath let loose the two seas.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: He has let loosed the two seas (the salt water and the sweet) meeting together.

19.author: Itani
19.text: He merged the two seas, converging together.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: He unleashed the two seas so that they merge together,

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: He has Maraja the two seas meeting together.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: He hath loosed the two seas. They meet.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: He merged the two seas, meeting each other.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: He has let forth the two seas, they meet together,

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: He released the two seas, meeting [side by side];

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: He has made the two oceans meet each other,

19.author: Shakir
19.text: He has made the two seas to flow freely (so that) they meet together:

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: He has set the two oceans in motion, converging together.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: He has let free the two bodies of flowing water, meeting together:

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: With an interstice between them which they cannot cross.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Whereas there is a barrier between them so they cannot encroach upon one another.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: between them a barrier they do not overpass.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: In-between the twain is a barrier which they pass not.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: Between them is a barrier which none of them can transgress.

20.author: Itani
20.text: Between them is a barrier, which they do not overrun.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: and yet there is a barrier between them which they may not overstep.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: Between them is a barrier which none of them can transgress.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: There is a barrier between them. They encroach not (one upon the other).

20.author: Qarai
20.text: There is a barrier between them, which they do not overstep.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: and between them is a barrier which they do not overpass.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: Between them is a barrier [so] neither of them transgresses.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: but has created a barrier between them so that they will not merge totally.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Between them is a barrier which they cannot pass.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Between them is a barrier, which they do not overrun.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Between them is a Barrier which they do not transgress:

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: Out of them come pearls and coral.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: Pearls and coral-stone come forth from them.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: From them come forth the pearl and the coral.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: There come forth from the twain the pearl and the coral.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: Out of them both come out pearl and coral.

22.author: Itani
22.text: From them emerge pearls and coral.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: From these seas come forth pearls and coral.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: Out of them both come out pearls and Al-Marjan.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: There cometh forth from both of them the pearl and coral-stone.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: From them emerge the pearl and the coral.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: Pearls and corals come from both.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: From both of them emerge pearl and coral.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: From the two oceans comes pearls and coral.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: There come forth from them pearls, both large and small.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: Pearls and corals come forth from both of them.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Out of them come Pearls and Coral:

23.author: Itani

23.text: Which of the wonders of your Lord's power will you twain – you men and jinn – then deny?

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny

23.author: Pickthall

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: His are the high-sailed vessels in deep ocean like the mountains.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: To Him only belong the sailing ships, raised above the sea like hills.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: His too are the ships that run, raised up in the sea like land-marks.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: His are the ships with elevated sails upon the sea like mountains.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: And His are the ships going and coming in the seas, like mountains.

24.author: Itani
24.text: His are the ships, raised above the sea like landmarks.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: His are the ships, towering on the sea like mountains.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: And His are Al-Jawar Al-Munsha'at, in the seas like A`lam.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: His are the ships displayed upon the sea, like banners.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: His are the sailing ships on the sea [appearing] like landmarks.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: Also, His are the ships that run, raised up like mountains upon the sea.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And to Him belong the ships [with sails] elevated in the sea like mountains.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: By His command, the ships with raised masts sail on the sea like mountains.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: And His are the ships reared aloft in the sea like mountains.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: His are the lofty ships, that rear aloft on the sea like mountains.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: And His are the Ships sailing smoothly through the seas, lofty as mountains:

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Walahu aljawari almunshaatu fee albahri kaalaAAlami

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: All that is on the earth is passing,

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: For everything on the earth is extinction.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: All that dwells upon the earth is perishing,

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Everyone that is thereon will Pass away.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: Whatsoever is on it (the earth) will perish.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: All that is on earth will perish,

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: Whatsoever is on it (the earth) will perish.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Everyone that is thereon will pass away;

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: All that live on it will perish.

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: Everyone upon the earth will perish,

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: Everyone on earth is destined to die.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: Everyone on it must pass away.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: All that is on the earth is doomed to perish,

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: All that is on earth will perish:

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: But abiding is the glory of your Lord, full of majesty and beneficence.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And everlasting is the Entity of your Lord, the Most Majestic and the Most Honourable.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: yet still abides the Face of thy Lord, majestic, splendid.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And there will remain the countenance of thine Lord, Owner of Majesty and Beneficence.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And the Face of your Lord full of Majesty and Honour will abide forever.

27.author: Itani
27.text: But will remain the Presence of your Lord, Full of Majesty and Splendor.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: only the Person of your Lord, full of majesty and splendour, will endure.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And the Face of your Lord Dhul-Jalal wal-Ikram will remain forever.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: There remaineth but the Countenance of thy Lord of Might and Glory.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: yet lasting is the majestic and munificent Face of your Lord.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: Yet the Face of your Lord will abide forever, Majestic and Splendid.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And there will remain the Face of your Lord, Owner of Majesty and Honor.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: Only the Supreme Essence of your Glorious and Gracious Lord will remain forever.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And there will endure for ever the person of your Lord, the Lord of glory and honor.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: while your Lord's own Self will remain full of majesty and glory.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: But will abide (for ever) the Face of thy Lord,- full of Majesty, Bounty and Honour.

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Wayabqa wajhu rabbika thoo aljalali waalikrami

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: All those there are in the heavens and the earth turn to Him with solicitation, intent on His purpose all the time.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: All those who are in the heavens and the earth seek only from Him; every day is an enterprise for Him.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: Whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth implore Him; every day He is upon some labour.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Of Him beggeth whosoever is in the heavens and the earth; every day He is in a new affair.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: Whosoever is in the heavens and on earth begs of Him (its needs from Him). Every day He has a matter to bring forth (such as giving honour to some, disgrace to some, life to some, death to some, etc.)!

29.author: Itani
29.text: Everyone in the heavens and the earth asks Him. Every day He is managing.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: All in the heavens and the earth entreat Him for their needs; a new, mighty task engages Him each day.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: Whosoever is in the heavens and on the earth begs of Him. Every day He is (engaged) in some affair!

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: All that are in the heavens and the earth entreat Him. Every day He exerciseth (universal) power.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: Everyone in the heavens and the earth asks Him. Every day He is engaged in some work.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: Whosoever is in the heaven and earth ask Him. Every day He is upon an affair (He reveals according to His Eternal determination).

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: Whoever is within the heavens and earth asks Him; every day He is bringing about a matter.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: Everyone in the heavens and the earth depends on Him. His task in preserving His creation is continuous.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: All those who are in the heavens and the earth ask of Him; every moment He is in a state (of glory).

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: Everyone in the heavens and on the earth entreats Him. Every day He manifests Himself in a new state.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: Of Him seeks (its need) every creature in the heavens and on earth: every day in (new) Splendour doth He (shine)!

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Yasaluhu man fee alssamawati waalardi kulla yawmin huwa fee shanin

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: We shall soon be free to turn to you, O weary caravans,

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: Disposing all works quickly We tend towards your account, O you two large groups!

31.author: Arberry
31.text: We shall surely attend to you at leisure, you weight and you weight!

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Anon We shall direct Our selves to you, O ye two classes.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: We shall attend to you, O you two classes (jinns and men)!

31.author: Itani
31.text: We will attend to you, O prominent two.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: O you twain, who are a burden (on the earth), We shall attend to you and call you to account.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: We shall attend to you, O Thaqalan!

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: We shall dispose of you, O ye two dependents (man and jinn).

31.author: Qarai
31.text: Soon We shall make Ourselves unoccupied for you, O you notable two!

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: We shall surely aim, O both burdened.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: We will attend to you, O prominent beings.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: Jinn and mankind, We shall certainly settle your accounts.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: Soon will We apply Ourselves to you, O you two armies.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: Soon We shall attend to you -- two big groups [of jinn and mankind].

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: Soon shall We settle your affairs, O both ye worlds!

32.text: How many favours of your Lord will you then deny?

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: Then which of the favours of your Lord will ye deny?

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: O society of jinns and men, cross the bounds of the heavens and the earth if you have the ability, then pass beyond them; but you cannot unless you acquire the law.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: O the groups of jinns and men, if you can cross the boundaries of the heavens and the earth, then cross them; wherever you may go, His is the kingdom!

33.author: Arberry
33.text: O tribe of jinn and of men, if you are able to pass through the confines of heaven and earth, pass through them! You shall not pass through except with an authority.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: O assembly of Jinn and man! if ye be able to pass out of the regions of the heavens and the earth, then pass out; ye can not pass out except with authority.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: O assembly of jinns and men! If you have power to pass beyond the zones of the heavens and the earth, then pass (them)! But you will never be able to pass them, except with authority (from Allah)!

33.author: Itani
33.text: O society of jinn and humans! If you can pass through the bounds of the heavens and the earth, go ahead and pass. But you will not pass except with authorization.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: O company of jinn and men, if you have the power to go beyond the bounds of the heavens and the earth, go beyond them! Yet you will be unable to go beyond them for that requires infinite power.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: O assembly of Jinn and men! If you are able to pass beyond the zones of the heavens and the earth, then pass beyond (them)! But you will never be able to pass them, except with authority (from Allah)!

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: O company of jinn and men, if ye have power to penetrate (all) regions of the heavens and the earth, then penetrate (them)! Ye will never penetrate them save with (Our) sanction.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: O company of jinn and humans! If you can pass through the confines of the heavens and the earth, then do pass through. But you will not pass through except by an authority [from Allah].

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: Tribe of jinn and of human, if you are able to penetrate the provinces of heaven and earth, pass through them! But you shall not pass through except with a power!

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: O company of jinn and mankind, if you are able to pass beyond the regions of the heavens and the earth, then pass. You will not pass except by authority [from Allah].

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: Jinn and mankind, if you can penetrate the diameters of the heavens and the earth, do so, but you cannot do so without power and authority.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: O assembly of the jinn and the men! If you are able to pass through the regions of the heavens and the earth, then pass through; you cannot pass through but with authority.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: O company of jinn and men! If you have the power to go beyond the realms of the heavens and the earth, pass beyond them: you cannot pass out but with [Our] authority.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: O ye assembly of Jinns and men! If it be ye can pass beyond the zones of the heavens and the earth, pass ye! not without authority shall ye be able to pass!

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Ya maAAshara aljinni waalinsi ini istataAAtum an tanfuthoo min aqtari alssamawati waalardi faonfuthoo la tanfuthoona illa bisultanin

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Let loose at you will be smokeless flames of fire so that you will not be able to defend yourselves.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: Flames of smokeless fire and black smoke without flames, will be let loose on you, so you will not be able to retaliate.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: Against you shall be loosed a flame of fire, and molten brass; and you shall not be helped.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: There shall be sent against you both flame of fire and smoke, and ye shall not be able to defend yourselves.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: There will be sent against you both, smokeless flames of fire and (molten) brass, and you will not be able to defend yourselves.

35.author: Itani
35.text: You will be bombarded with flares of fire and brass, and you will not succeed.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: (If you so venture) a flame of fire and smoke shall be lashed at you, which you shall be unable to withstand.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: There will be sent against you both, Shuwaz of fire and Nuhas, and you will not be able to defend yourselves.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: There will be sent, against you both, heat of fire and flash of brass, and ye will not escape.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: There will be unleashed upon you a flash of fire and a smoke; then you will not be able to help one another.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Flames of fire and molten copper shall be loosed against you, and you shall not be helped.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: There will be sent upon you a flame of fire and smoke, and you will not defend yourselves.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: Flames of fire and molten brass will be released against you and you will not be able to protect yourselves.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: The flames of fire and smoke will be sent on you two, then you will not be able to defend yourselves.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: Flames of fire and molten brass shall be sent against both of you, and you will not be able to defend yourselves.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: On you will be sent (O ye evil ones twain!) a flame of fire (to burn) and a smoke (to choke): no defence will ye have:

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Yursalu AAalaykuma shuwathun min narin wanuhasun fala tantasirani

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: When the sky will split asunder, and turn rosy like the dregs of annointing oil,

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: And when the heaven will split, it will appear like a rose painted red.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: And when heaven is split asunder, and turns crimson like red leather --

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: And when the heaven will be rent in sunder and will become rosy like Unto red hide.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: Then when the heaven is rent asunder, and it becomes rosy or red like red-oil, or red hide.

37.author: Itani
37.text: When the sky splits apart, and becomes rose, like paint.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: (What will happen) when the heaven will be split asunder and will become crimson like leather?

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: Then when the heaven is rent asunder, and it becomes Wardah like Dihan.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: And when the heaven splitteth asunder and becometh rosy like red hide -

37.author: Qarai
37.text: When the sky is split open, and turns crimson like tanned leather.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: When the heaven splits asunder and turns crimson, like red leather.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: And when the heaven is split open and becomes rose-colored like oil -

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: (On the Day of Judgment) when the heavens are rent asunder they will have a flowery color and flow like oil.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: And when the heaven is rent asunder, and then becomes red like red hide.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: When the sky is rent asunder, and becomes red like red leather,

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: When the sky is rent asunder, and it becomes red like ointment:

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Faitha inshaqqati alssamao fakanat wardatan kaalddihani

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: Which of the favours of your Lord will you then deny?

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: So O men and jinns! Which favour of your Lord will you deny?

38.author: Arberry
38.text: O which of our Lord's bounties will you and you deny?

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: Whfch, then, of the benefits of your Lord will ye twain belie!

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: Then which of the Blessings of your Lord will you both (jinns and men) deny?

38.author: Itani
38.text: So which of your Lord’s marvels will you deny?

38.author: Maududi
38.text: Which of your Lord's powers will you twain – you men and jinn – then deny?

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: Which is it, of the favours of your Lord, that ye deny? -

38.author: Qarai
38.text: So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny?

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: Which favors of your Lord will you both belie?

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? -

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: (Jinn and mankind) - which of the favors of your Lord do you then deny?

38.author: Shakir
38.text: Which then of the bounties of your Lord will you deny?

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: which of your Lord's wonders would you deny?

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: Then which of the favours of your Lord will ye deny?

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: Neither man nor jinn will be questioned on that day about his sin.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: On that day no sinner will be questioned about his sins, from men or from jinns.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: on that day none shall be questioned about his sin, neither man nor jinn.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: Of his sin will be questioned that Day neither man nor jinn.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: So on that Day no question will be asked of man or jinn as to his sin, (because they have already been known from their faces either white or black).

39.author: Itani
39.text: On that Day, no human and no jinn will be asked about his sins.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: On that Day there will be no need to ask either men or jinn about their sins.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: So, on that Day he will not be questioned about his sin, (neither) human nor Jinn.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: On that day neither man nor jinni will be questioned of his sin.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: On that day neither humans will be questioned about their sins nor jinn.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: On that Day, neither human nor jinn shall be asked about his sin.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: Then on that Day none will be asked about his sin among men or jinn.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: On that day there will be no need to ask mankind or jinn about his sin

39.author: Shakir
39.text: So on that day neither man nor jinni shall be asked about his sin.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: For, on that Day, neither man nor jinn will be questioned about his sins.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: On that Day no question will be asked of man or Jinn as to his sin.

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Fayawmaithin la yusalu AAan thanbihi insun wala jannun

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: The sinners will be recognised by their marks, and seized by the forelock and their feet.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: The criminals will be recognised from their faces, so will be caught by their forelocks and feet, and thrown into hell.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: The sinners shall be known by their mark, and they shall be seized by their forelocks and their feet.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: The culprits will be recognised by their marks, and will be lain hold of by the forelocks and the feet.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: The Mujrimun (polytheists, criminals, sinners, etc.) will be known by their marks (black faces), and they will be seized by their forelocks and their feet.

41.author: Itani
41.text: The guilty will be recognized by their marks; they will be taken by the forelocks and the feet.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: The culprits shall be known by their marks, and shall be seized by their forelocks and their feet.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: The criminals will be known by their marks, and they will be seized by their foreheads and feet.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: The guilty will be known by their marks, and will be taken by the forelocks and the feet.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: The guilty will be recognized by their mark; so they will be seized by their forelocks and feet.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: The sinners shall be known by their mark, they shall be seized by their forelocks and their feet.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: The criminals will be known by their marks, and they will be seized by the forelocks and the feet.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: The guilty ones will be recognized by their faces and will be seized by their forelocks and feet.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: The guilty shall be recognized by their marks, so they shall be seized by the forelocks and the feet.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: The guilty shall be recognized by their marks so they shall be seized by their forelocks and their feet.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: (For) the sinners will be known by their marks: and they will be seized by their forelocks and their feet.

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: YuAArafu almujrimoona biseemahum fayukhathu bialnnawasee waalaqdami

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: This is Hell the sinners called a lie.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: This is hell, which the criminals deny.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: This is Gehenna, that sinners cried lies to;

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Yonder is the Hell which the culprits belied.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: This is Hell which the Mujrimun (polytheists, criminals, sinners, etc.) denied.

43.author: Itani
43.text: This is Hell that the guilty denied.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: (It will be said): “This is the Hell that the culprits had cried lies to.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: This is the Hell which the criminals denied.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: This is hell which the guilty deny.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: ‘This is the hell which the guilty would deny!’

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: This is Gehenna (Hell) which the sinners belied,

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: This is Hell, which the criminals deny.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: (The guilty ones will be told), "This is hell which the sinful ones denied".

43.author: Shakir
43.text: This is the hell which the guilty called a lie.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: This is the Hell which the guilty called a lie.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: This is the Hell which the Sinners deny:

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Hathihi jahannamu allatee yukaththibu biha almujrimoona

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: They will go round and around between it and boiling water.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: They shall keep going back and forth between it and the extremely hot boiling water.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: they shall go round between it and between hot, boiling water.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Going round between it and balling water fierce.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: They will go between it (Hell) and the boiling hot water!

44.author: Itani
44.text: They circulate between it and between a seething bath.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: They will keep circling around between Hell and boiling water.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: They will go between it and Hamim An!

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: They go circling round between it and fierce, boiling water.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: They shall circuit between it and boiling hot water.

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: they shall go round between it, and between a hot, boiling water.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: They will go around between it and scalding water, heated [to the utmost degree].

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: They will run around in blazing fire and boiling water.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: Round about shall they go between it and hot, boiling water.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: They will go round between its flames and boiling water.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: In its midst and in the midst of boiling hot water will they wander round!

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: But for him who lived in awe of the sublimity of his Lord, there will be two gardens --

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: And for one who fears to stand before his Lord, are two Gardens.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: But such as fears the Station of his Lord, for them shall be two gardens --

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: And for him who dreadeth the standing before his Lord will be two Gardens.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: But for him who [the true believer of Islamic Monotheism who performs all the duties ordained by Allah and His Messenger Muhammad SAW, and keeps away (abstain) from all kinds of sin and evil deeds prohibited in Islam and] fears the standing before his Lord, there will be two Gardens (i.e. in Paradise).

46.author: Itani
46.text: But for him who feared the standing of his Lord are two gardens.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: For any who fears to stand before his Lord are two Gardens.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: But for him who fears the standing before his Lord, there will be two Gardens.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: But for him who feareth the standing before his Lord there are two gardens.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: For him who stands in awe of his Lord will be two gardens.

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: And for he who fears the standing (before) his Lord there are two Gardens.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: But for he who has feared the position of his Lord are two gardens -

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: Those who fear their Lord will have two gardens

46.author: Shakir
46.text: And for him who fears to stand before his Lord are two gardens.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: There are two gardens for one who fears standing before his Lord.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: But for such as fear the time when they will stand before (the Judgment Seat of) their Lord, there will be two Gardens-

52.author: Arberry
52.text: therein of every fruit two kinds --

52.author: Daryabadi

52.author: Hilali & Khan

54.text: Reclining upon thrones that are lined with brocade, with the fruit of both Gardens close enough to be picked from under.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: reclining upon couches lined with brocade, the fruits of the gardens nigh to gather --

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: Reclining on carpets whereof the linings will be of brocade; and the fruit of the two Gardens shall be near at hand.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: Reclining upon the couches lined with silk brocade, and the fruits of the two Gardens will be near at hand.

54.author: Itani
54.text: Reclining on furnishings lined with brocade, and the fruits of the two gardens are near at hand.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: They shall recline on couches lined with brocade, and within reach shall hang the fruits of the two Gardens.

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: Reclining upon the couches lined with Istabraq, and the fruits of the two Gardens will be near at hand.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: Reclining upon couches lined with silk brocade, the fruit of both the gardens near to hand.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: [They will be] reclining on beds lined with green silk. And the fruit of the two gardens will be near at hand.

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: (They shall) recline on couches lined with brocade, and the fruits of the Gardens will be near at hand.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: [They are] reclining on beds whose linings are of silk brocade, and the fruit of the two gardens is hanging low.

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: (The dwellers of Paradise) will recline on couches lined with silk brocade and it will be easy to reach the ripe fruits from the two gardens.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: Reclining on beds, the inner coverings of which are of silk brocade; and the fruits of the two gardens shall be within reach.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: They will recline upon carpets lined with rich brocade; and the fruits of both these gardens will be within easy reach.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: They will recline on Carpets, whose inner linings will be of rich brocade: the Fruit of the Gardens will be near (and easy of reach).

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Muttakieena AAala furushin batainuha min istabraqin wajana aljannatayni danin

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: In them maidens with averted glances, undeflowered by man or by jinn before them, --

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: Upon thrones are the women who do not gaze at men except their husbands, and before them, are untouched by any man or jinn.

56.author: Arberry
56.text: therein maidens restraining their glances, untouched before them by any man or jinn --

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: Therein shall be those of refraining looks whom before them hath deflowered neither man nor jinn.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: Wherein both will be those (maidens) restraining their glances upon their husbands, whom no man or jinn yatmithhunna (has opened their hymens with sexual intercourse) before them.

56.author: Itani
56.text: In them are maidens restraining their glances, untouched before by any man or jinn.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: In the midst of these shall be maidens with modest, restrained glances; maidens whom no man or jinn has ever touched before.

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: Wherein both will be Qasirat At-Tarf, whom never deflowered a human before nor Jinn.

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: Therein are those of modest gaze, whom neither man nor jinni will have touched before them.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: In them are maidens of restrained glances, whom no human has touched before, nor jinn.

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: Therein are maidens who restrain their glances, whom neither human nor jinn have touched before.

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: In them are women limiting [their] glances, untouched before them by man or jinni -

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: There will be bashful maidens untouched by mankind or jinn before.

56.author: Shakir
56.text: In them shall be those who restrained their eyes; before them neither man nor jinni shall have touched them.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: Therein are maidens of modest gaze, whom neither a man nor a jinn had ever touched before them.

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: In them will be (Maidens), chaste, restraining their glances, whom no man or Jinn before them has touched;-

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Feehinna qasiratu alttarfi lam yatmithhunna insun qablahum wala jannun

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: They are houris (maidens of Paradise), hidden from view, in pavilions.

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: Houris (beautiful, fair females) restrained in pavilions;

72.author: Maududi
72.text: There shall be maidens sheltered in tents.

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: Hur (beautiful, fair females) guarded in pavilions;

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: Maidens (of Paradise, Houris) in cloistered cool pavilions.

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: - with big, black and white beautiful eyes, dwelling in tents.

72.author: Shakir
72.text: Pure ones confined to the pavilions.

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: [There the blessed will live with their] pure companions sheltered in pavilions.

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: Companions restrained (as to their glances), in (goodly) pavilions;-

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: Undeflowered by man or by jinn before them, --

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: Untouched by any man or jinn, before them.

74.author: Arberry
74.text: untouched before them by any man or jinn --

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: There hath deflowered them neither man nor jinn.

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: Whom no man or jinn yatmithhunna (has opened their hymens with sexual intercourse) before them.

74.author: Itani
74.text: Whom no human has touched before, nor jinn.

74.author: Maududi
74.text: No man or jinn ever touched them before.

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: Whom never deflowered a human before nor Jinn.

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: Whom neither man nor jinni will have touched before them -

74.author: Qarai
74.text: Whom no human has touched before, nor jinn.

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: Neither human nor jinn will have touched them before.

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: Untouched before them by man or jinni -

74.author: Sarwar
74.text: - untouched by jinn or mankind before.

74.author: Shakir
74.text: Man has not touched them before them nor jinni.

74.author: Wahiduddin Khan
74.text: Whom neither a man or jinn had ever touched before them.

74.author: Yusuf Ali
74.text: Whom no man or Jinn before them has touched;-

74.author: Transliteration
74.text: Lam yatmithhunna insun qablahum wala jannun

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: Blessed be the name of your Lord, full of majesty and beneficience.

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: Most Auspicious is the name of your Lord, the Most Majestic and the Most Honourable.

78.author: Arberry
78.text: Blessed be the Name of thy Lord, majestic, splendid.

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: Blest be the name of thine Lord, Owner Of Majesty and Beneficence!

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: Blessed be the Name of your Lord (Allah), the Owner of Majesty and Honour.

78.author: Itani
78.text: Blessed be the name of your Lord, Full of Majesty and Splendor.

78.author: Maududi
78.text: Blessed be the name of your Lord, the Lord of Majesty and Glory.

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: Blessed be the Name of your Lord, Dhil-Jalali wal-Ikram.

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: Blessed be the name of thy Lord, Mighty and glorious!

78.author: Qarai
78.text: Blessed is the Name of your Lord, the Majestic and the Munificent!

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: Blessed be the Name of your Lord, Majestic, Splendid.

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: Blessed is the name of your Lord, Owner of Majesty and Honor.

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: Blessed is the Name of your Lord, the Lord of Glory and Grace.

78.author: Shakir
78.text: Blessed be the name of your Lord, the Lord of Glory and Honor!

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: Blessed be your Lord's name, full of glory and majesty!

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: Blessed be the name of thy Lord, full of Majesty, Bounty and Honour.

78.text: Tabaraka ismu rabbika thee aljalali waalikrami
